job_analysis.job_description:
We are looking for a detail-oriented HR Analyst to support our HR department with data analysis and reporting. You'll work on tracking key HR metrics like employee turnover, recruitment efficiency, and compensation trends to support strategic decisions.
Key Responsibilities:
Analyze HR data and create dashboards
Track and report KPIs (e.g., time-to-hire, attrition)
Maintain HR systems and ensure data accuracy
Support workforce planning and recruitment analysis
Profile:
Bachelor’s/Master’s in HR, Business, or related field
Strong Excel and analytical skills
Familiar with HRIS (SAP, Odoo, etc.)
French and English (B2 level)
Organized and data-driven
critical_requirements: 3+ years of direct experience as HR Analyst or similar data-centric HR role
Hands-on experience with HR data analysis
Creating reports/dashboards for HR metrics (turnover, recruitment, compensation)
Bachelor’s/Master’s in HR, Business, or related field
B2 level proficiency in French and English
preferred_requirements: Advanced tools (Power BI, Tableau, Python, R)
job_analysis.experience_required: 3+ years in HR Analyst or highly similar data-centric HR role, direct experience with HR data analysis and dashboard/report creation
job_analysis.education_required: Bachelor's or Master's in HR, Business, or a related quantitative field

candidate_overview.education_level: Engineering degree in Business Intelligence and Information Systems (in progress), Bachelor's degree in General Science Technologies

candidate_evidence: HR Data Analyst at SAIPH since 01/2024 (6+ months)
Business Intelligence Developer Intern at SAIPH, with duties including HR data analysis and dashboard creation
Experience with dashboards for Absenteeism, Productivity, Workforce, Salary, and Social Works
Supported recruitment and employee management analytics
rubric_evaluation.score_justification: Candidate has approximately 1 year of direct HR Analyst/data-centric HR experience, with relevant responsibilities in HR metrics, dashboards, and recruitment analytics, but does not meet the 3+ year requirement. Therefore, partial points awarded.

candidate_evidence: Proficient in Excel; key responsibilities include using Excel for data quality checks and visualization
Advanced skills in Power BI, SQL, Python (Pandas)
Hands-on with HR analytics dashboards and reporting
No explicit mention of HRIS such as SAP, Odoo, or Workday
English: Full professional proficiency; French: Limited working proficiency (not B2)
rubric_evaluation.score_justification: Candidate describes proficiency in Excel, but advanced Excel skills (Pivot Tables, VLOOKUPs, etc.) not explicitly stated. No named HRIS systems per job requirements; advanced analytical tool skills shown; French below B2. Thus, earns partial points.

candidate_evidence: Currently pursuing Engineering degree in Business Intelligence and Information Systems
Holds Bachelor’s degree in General Science Technologies
No mention of specific HR-related coursework or HR/data analytics certifications
rubric_evaluation.score_justification: Bachelor’s and pursuing engineering in a related quantitative field; however, not explicit HR or business major, and no HR certifications. Partial points appropriate; degree is related to analytics.

candidate_evidence: Enhanced accuracy in payroll and training expense analytics
Provided real-time HR insights via dashboards
Streamlined HR reporting
Improved employee engagement analysis
rubric_evaluation.score_justification: Achievements describe improvements and impact, but lack quantifiable results (e.g., percentage/time saved). Verbs demonstrate ownership and impact orientation. Partial points assigned.

candidate_evidence: Described as collaborative, detail-oriented in actions, stakeholder communication skills
Roles and achievements demonstrate organization and teamwork
No direct use of all job description keywords ('data-driven', 'detail-oriented') in resume wording
rubric_evaluation.score_justification: Soft skills are implied through experience, but the explicit alignment and mirroring of job description language is limited. Resume structure is clear, indicating some organization. Partial points appropriate.

candidate_evidence: Advanced Power BI skills indicated in dashboard design and real-time reporting
Use of Python for ETL and data processing
No predictive modeling or R/statistical programming
rubric_evaluation.score_justification: Candidate exceeds basic requirements through advanced data visualization and Python ETL skills (Power BI, Python). Bonus points fully assigned.

missing_skills: Explicit advanced Excel operations (Pivot Tables, VLOOKUPs)

experience_match.required_experience: 3+ years of direct HR Analyst/data-centric HR role

education_match.required_education: Bachelor's or Master's in HR, Business, or related quantitative field
education_match.candidate_education: Bachelor's degree in General Science Technologies; Engineering degree (Business Intelligence, in progress)

critical_missing_requirements: 3+ years direct HR Analyst/data-centric HR experience
Verifiable HRIS system expertise (SAP, Odoo, Workday)
key_strengths: Strong Power BI and dashboard development experience
Experience with Python and data warehousing/ETL in HR context
areas_for_improvement: Gain more direct HR Analyst experience (target 3+ years)
Develop advanced Excel operations (Pivot Tables, VLOOKUPs, etc.)
Gain hands-on experience with leading HRIS platforms
unique_value_propositions: Advanced data visualization and automation with Python and Power BI in HR setting
Experience designing comprehensive HR dashboards quickly
Emerging expertise at intersection of business intelligence and HR analytics
risk_factors: Below required experience threshold may affect ability to independently own analysis scope
No direct HRIS platform experience limits integration with some HR processes
French proficiency below client requirement level

final_recommendation.primary_reason: While candidate is strong in HR analytics, data visualization, and technical tools, lacks minimum required years of experience, HRIS system background, and B2 French proficiency.
supporting_factors: Strong technical and analytical skills with practical HR analytics application
Rapid growth and learning demonstrated in early career trajectory
Tangible impact via dashboard and reporting improvements
conditions_if_applicable: Willingness to invest in further HRIS and advanced Excel training
Assignment to team with strong HR process/HRIS knowledge
next_steps: Assess technical skills in Excel and HRIS in interview
Discuss HR process understanding and ability to upscale quickly
Verify achievements and independence with references

detailed_feedback.for_candidate: You have demonstrated strong growth in HR analytics and data tools (Power BI, Python, SQL). To be highly competitive for more senior HR Analyst roles, focus on gaining deeper hands-on experience with leading HRIS systems (e.g., SAP, Odoo), mastering advanced Excel functionalities, and elevating your French to the required B2 level. Continue developing quantifiable, results-oriented achievements linked to HR business outcomes.
detailed_feedback.for_hiring_manager: Candidate brings strong data analysis/visualization capacity, high technical acumen, and a proven ability to support HR with actionable insights. However, experience is below the strict minimum, and no direct HRIS platform skills present. If technical growth and upskilling are high priorities, and HRIS/French can be trained, this candidate could be a high-upside investment.
interview_focus_areas: Ability to handle end-to-end HR analytics projects
reference_check_priorities: Candidate’s role in independent HR analytics/dashboards
Proven reliability in data quality and HR reporting
Capacity to quickly adapt to unfamiliar HR tools/systems
